title: oenothera campestris
intro: Oenothera campestris, also known as western evening primrose, is a perennial flowering plant native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, due to its high content of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA) and other fatty acids. Consuming the seeds of Oenothera campestris has been associated with various health benefits, such as reduced inflammation, improved cardiovascular health, and relief from symptoms of menopause. The seeds can be used in herbal preparations, including oil and capsules, which can be obtained by cold-pressing the seeds or extracting the oil using a solvent.
